# ENS Components by Namespace
# https://enscomponents.com

User-agent: *
Allow: /
AI-Training: allowed
AI-Retrieval: allowed

## About

ENS Components (https://enscomponents.com) is the official interactive documentation and live
playground for @thenamespace/ens-components — a production-ready React component library for
building ENS (Ethereum Name Service) experiences. It ships five drop-in components for ENS name
registration, profile record editing, and subname issuance, all with built-in wallet integration
via wagmi and viem.

## Package

- npm: @thenamespace/ens-components
- GitHub: https://github.com/thenamespace/ens-components
- License: MIT
- Developer portal: https://dev.namespace.ninja

## Installation

```bash
npm install @thenamespace/ens-components wagmi viem @tanstack/react-query
```

Import the stylesheet once at your app root:

```tsx
import "@thenamespace/ens-components/styles";
```

Wrap your app with wagmi's WagmiProvider and QueryClientProvider (see Quick Start on the site).

## Components

### 1. EnsNameRegistrationForm
Full .eth name registration flow: search → commit → wait → register.

Key props:
- name?: string — pre-fill a search name
- isTestnet?: boolean — use Sepolia (default: false)
- referrer?: Address — referrer address for fee sharing
- noBorder?: boolean — remove card border
- title?: string — override card title
- subtitle?: string — override card subtitle
- bannerImage?: string — custom banner image URL
- hideBanner?: boolean — hide the top banner
- avatarUploadDomain?: string — SIWE domain for avatar uploads
- onRegistrationSuccess?: (result: RegistrationSuccessData) => void
- onRegistrationStart?: (name: string) => void
- onConnectWallet?: () => void
- onClose?: (isSuccess: boolean) => void

### 2. EnsRecordsForm
Full profile record editor for an ENS name. Fetches current on-chain records and lets the user
edit text records, addresses, avatar, and contenthash, then submits a single multicall transaction.

Key props:
- name: string — full ENS name (e.g. "alice.eth")
- existingRecords: EnsRecords — current records (fetch with useENSResolver hook)
- isTestnet?: boolean
- resolverChainId?: number — override resolver chain
- resolverAddress?: Address — override resolver contract
- noBorder?: boolean
- avatarUploadDomain?: string
- txConfirmations?: number — confirmations to wait for (default: 1)
- onRecordsUpdated?: (diff: EnsRecordsDiff) => void
- onTransactionSent?: (hash: Hash) => void
- onCancel?: () => void
- onGreat?: () => void

### 3. SelectRecordsForm
Standalone record selector with no wallet requirement. Renders the same editor UI but calls back
with the updated EnsRecords object — useful when you manage the submit step yourself.

Key props:
- records: EnsRecords — current record state
- onRecordsUpdated: (records: EnsRecords) => void — called on every change
- actionButtons?: React.ReactNode — inject custom buttons at the bottom
- avatarUpload?: AvatarUploadContext — enable avatar/header image uploads

### 4. OffchainSubnameForm
Gasless offchain subname creation via the Namespace CCIP-Read gateway (EIP-3668). No wallet
transaction required for end users.

Key props:
- offchainManager: OffchainClient — client instance from @thenamespace/offchain-manager
- name: string — parent ENS name (e.g. "myname.eth")
- label?: string — pre-fill the subname label
- isTestnet?: boolean
- title?: string
- subtitle?: string
- hideTitle?: boolean
- avatarUploadDomain?: string
- onSubnameCreated?: (data: OffchainSubnameCreatedData) => void
- onSubnameUpdated?: (data: OffchainSubnameCreatedData) => void
- onCancel?: () => void

### 5. SubnameMintForm
Onchain subname minting under a Namespace-activated ENS name. Looks up price, lets user pick
records, then submits a wallet transaction.

Key props:
- parentName: string — parent ENS name
- label?: string — pre-fill the subname label
- isTestnet?: boolean
- title?: string
- subtitle?: string
- avatarUploadDomain?: string
- txConfirmations?: number
- onSubnameMinted?: (data: SubnameMintedData) => void
- onSuccess?: (data: MintSuccessData) => void
- onConnectWallet?: () => void
- onCancel?: () => void

## Key TypeScript Types

```ts
interface EnsRecords {
  addresses: EnsAddressRecord[];
  texts: EnsTextRecord[];
  contenthash?: EnsContenthashRecord;
}

interface EnsTextRecord { key: string; value: string; }
interface EnsAddressRecord { coinType: number; value: string; }
interface EnsContenthashRecord {
  protocol: ContenthashProtocol;
  value: string;
}

enum ContenthashProtocol {
  Ipfs = "ipfs", Onion = "onion3", Arweave = "arweave",
  Skynet = "skynet", Swarm = "swarm",
}

type ChainName = "eth" | "arb" | "base" | "bitcoin" | "matic" | "op" | "sol" | "zora" | "celo";
```

## CSS Custom Properties (Theming)

Override these on `:root` or `[data-theme="dark"]` to theme the components:

```css
:root {
  --ns-font-family: "Satoshi", system-ui, sans-serif;
  --ns-radius-sm: 4px;
  --ns-radius-md: 8px;
  --ns-radius-lg: 12px;
  --ns-color-bg: #ffffff;
  --ns-color-bg-accent: #f4f4f4;
  --ns-color-bg-accent-hover: #d9d9d9;
  --ns-color-bg-darker: #e2e2e2;
  --ns-color-fg: #111827;
  --ns-color-muted: #6b7280;
  --ns-color-primary: #1f1f1f;
  --ns-color-primary-contrast: #ffffff;
  --ns-color-border: #e5e7eb;
  --ns-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.04);
}
```

## Framework Compatibility

Works in any React 19+ app. Compatible with Next.js (App Router and Pages Router), Remix, Vite,
and Create React App. For SSR frameworks, mark the component tree as a Client Component or use
dynamic imports with ssr: false, since components use wagmi wallet hooks.

## What this site contains

- Live interactive demos of each component
- Copy-paste code examples
- Prop editor for every component
- Quick-start installation guide
- FAQ covering common integration questions

## Full API reference

See https://enscomponents.com/llms-full.txt for the complete API reference with all prop tables,
full TypeScript types, CSS variable listing, error handling notes, and integration recipes.
